headline: CCI approves acquisition of 100% of the equity share capital of NCR Atleos Corporation (Target) by The Brink's Company (Acquirer)
fullText:
Competition Commission of India

CCI approves acquisition of 100% of the equity share capital of NCR Atleos Corporation (Target) by The Brink's Company (Acquirer)

22 JUL 2026 8:57PM by PIB Delhi

Competition Commission of India (CCI) has approved the acquisition of 100% of the equity share capital of NCR Atleos Corporation (Target) by The Brink's Company (Acquirer).

The Proposed Combination entails the acquisition of 100% of the equity share capital of the Target by the Acquirer. Post completion, the Target shall be wholly owned and controlled by the Acquirer.

The Acquirer is a global provider of cash and valuables management, digital retail solutions and ATM managed services, operating in over 100 countries. In India, the Acquirer principally provides cash replenishment services and ancillary first-line maintenance service through its subsidiary Brink's India Private Limited.

The Target is a financial technology company providing self-directed banking solutions globally. In India, the Target's activities principally comprise: (i) manufacture and supply of ATM hardware, including through its Chennai manufacturing facility; (ii) development and licensing of ATM software; (iii) ATM managed services, including monitoring, help-desk and transaction processing; and (iv) ATM maintenance services, i.e., first-line maintenance and second line maintenance.

Detailed order of the Commission will follow.

(Release ID: 2288055)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has approved the acquisition of 100 % of the equity share capital of NCR Atleos Corporation by The Brink’s Company, effective after completion of the transaction.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Approval date:** 22 July 2026 (PRIianchi Bulletin ID 2288055).  
- **Transaction:** Full acquisition of 100 % of NCR Atleos Corporation’s equity.  
- **Post‑completion ownership:**ŭNCR Atleos will be wholly owned and controlled by The Brink’s Company.  
- **Acquirer profile:** Global provider of cash and valuables management, digital retail solutions, and ATM‑managed services; operating in over 100 countries; in India it delivers cash replenishment and first‑line maintenance through its subsidiary Brink’s India Private Limited.  
- **Target profile:** Financial‑technology company specializing in self‑directed banking solutions.  
- **Target’s Indian operations:**  
  - Manufacture and supply of ATM hardware at a Chennai facility.  
  - Development and licensing of ATM software.  
  - ATM managed services, including monitoring, help‑desk, and transaction processing.  
  ولنَ—ATM maintenance services (first‑line and second‑line).

headline: Ministry of Coal Releases 'AAROH': India's First Report on Scientific Mine Closure and Repurposing Practices
fullText:
Ministry of Coal

Ministry of Coal Releases 'AAROH': India's First Report on Scientific Mine Closure and Repurposing Practices

Ministry of Coal also Signs Implementation Agreement with Germany's GIZ to Boost Scientific Mine Closure Cooperation

Coal Minister Inaugurates Coal NEER Plants, Turning Mine Discharge into Safe Drinking Water

BCCL, JRDA Sign Tripartite MoUs with Hindalco and Rajdhani Universal Fabrics for Jharia Rehabilitation and Skill Development

22 JUL 2026 8:54PM by PIB Delhi

The Ministry of Coal today released ‘AAROH: Annual Report on Mine Closure’ at the Oberoi, New Delhi, highlighting India’s progress in adopting scientific approaches to mine closure, ecological restoration, and sustainable post-mining land utilisation.

The event was graced by Shri G. Kishan Reddy, Union Minister of Coal and Mines; Dr. Jitendra Singh, Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Science & Technology, Earth Sciences and Minister of State for the Prime Minister's Office, Personnel, Public Grievances, Pensions, Atomic Energy and Space; Shri Satish Chandra Dubey, Union Minister of State for Coal and Mines; H.E. Dr Philipp Ackermann, German Ambassador; Dr. Ewa Suwara, Deputy Head of the EU Delegation; Shri Vikram Dev Dutt, Secretary, Ministry of Coal; Shri Sanoj Kumar Jha, Additional Secretary, Ministry of Coal and Shri Sajeesh Kumar N., Coal Controller. The program witnessed participation from other senior officials of Central and State Governments, coal companies, international organisations, industry, academia, and other stakeholders associated with sustainable mining and post-mining development.

The Union Minister of Coal and Mines Shri G Kishan Reddy inaugurated the Exhibition on Mine Closure and Mine Repurposing, showcasing innovative initiatives undertaken by Coal India Limited and its subsidiaries, NLC India Limited, Singareni Collieries Company Limited (SCCL), NTPC and private mining organisations. The exhibition highlighted successful practices in scientific reclamation, ecological restoration, mine repurposing, community development, and sustainable utilisation of post-mining land.

The Ministers formally released ‘AAROH – Annual Report on Mine Closure’, followed by the screening of a documentary film showcasing the publication. The AAROH, developed by the Coal Controller Organisation, presents India’s first comprehensive documentation of scientific mine closure and repurposing practices. The report documents the transformative journey of 42 scientifically closed coal mines, showcasing structured reclamation processes, ecological restoration initiatives, sustainable post-mining land utilisation, and community-oriented interventions that have contributed to the creation of environmentally restored and socially beneficial landscapes.

Each mine profile featured in AAROH presents key closure initiatives, post-mining land-use outcomes, before-and-after visual documentation, and QR code-enabled documentary videos capturing the transformation journey of the respective mine sites. The publication serves as a knowledge repository, highlighting successful models and providing valuable insights for future mine closure and repurposing efforts.

The report also captures the evolution of India’s mine closure framework and highlights key initiatives of the Ministry of Coal, including the RECLAIM Framework, L.I.V.E.S. Framework, SUVIKALP – Mine Repurposing Tool, and SUVIKALP Samvaad, which promote scientific assessment, stakeholder participation, knowledge exchange, and sustainable post-mining land use.

Another highlight of the event was the signing of the Implementation Agreement between the Ministry of Coal and Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) for technical cooperation on ‘To-be closed mines’. The agreement aims to strengthen collaboration in scientific mine closure, mine repurposing, technical capacity building, knowledge exchange, and adoption of global best practices, further strengthening India-Germany cooperation in the mining sector. The project has been commissioned by the Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ) and is co-financed by the European Union, with a total project volume of EUR 10 million until 2030. The cooperation will take place over the next four years focusing on developing innovative and sustainable approaches for the closure and repurposing of coal mines in India. This investment by Germany and the European Union reflects Team Europe’s strong partnership with India to deepen cooperation on sustainable economic development, a priority reaffirmed at the 16th India–EU Summit and the visit of German Chancellor to India earlier this year.

The Union Minister also virtually inaugurated Coal NEER Plants established by Coal India Limited through its subsidiaries—Bharat Coking Coal Limited (BCCL), Central Coalfields Limited (CCL), Mahanadi Coalfields Limited (MCL), and South Eastern Coalfields Limited (SECL). The initiative is aimed at strengthening access to safe and sustainable drinking water facilities in coal-bearing regions and improving the quality of life of communities residing in mining areas. Aligning with the Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi’s vision of ‘Waste to Wealth’, these plants purify mine water and markets it as safe, high-quality bottled drinking water under the brand name ‘Coal Neer.’ These projects use advanced treatment methods including RO, UV and CDI technologies to provide safe, clean, and affordable drinking water to nearby villages and local communities. Apart from providing affordable bottled drinking water, these projects also offer sustainable livelihood opportunities for Self Help Groups from nearby villages.

The event also witnessed the signing of tripartite Memorandums of Understanding (MoUs) by Bharat Coking Coal Limited (BCCL) and Jharia Rehabilitation and Development Authority (JRDA) with Hindalco Industries Limited and Rajdhani Universal Fabrics Private Limited. These partnerships will facilitate coordinated efforts towards rehabilitation, infrastructure development, community welfare, and socio-economic development in the Jharia coalfield region. The MoU signed between BCCL, JRDA and Hindalco aims to establish an Industry-Linked Skill Centre that will equip the youth of the Dhanbad region with industry-relevant skills to work in world-class apparel manufacturing units. The MoU signed between BCCL, JRDA and Rajdhani Universal Fabrics Pvt Ltd aims to establish a world-class integrated technical textile manufacturing ecosystem, which will generate large-scale employment and strengthen skill development.

In his keynote address, Union Minister of Coal and Mines Shri G Kishan Reddy, mentioned that under the dynamic leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, India has fundamentally transformed the philosophy of mine closure, viewing it not as the end of mining but as the beginning of new opportunities for people, communities and sustainable development. He described scientific mine closure as a national priority and a key pillar of the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047, adding that India has scientifically closed 42 coal mines, including 33 in the last year alone, and has set an ambitious target of scientifically closing 147 more mines over the next two to three years. He highlighted that the Ministry has built a comprehensive ecosystem for mine closure through the RECLAIM Framework, the SUVIKALP digital platform, the A.R.T.H.A. Framework, and District Collector-led Mine Closure Advisory Committees, while the Mine Closure Guidelines, 2025 place people and sustainability at the heart of mine closure. The Minister further informed that, for the first time, 25 per cent of the approved mine closure cost has been earmarked for community development, creating an investment potential of nearly ₹10,000 crore over the next decade. He also announced that the Government has created a dedicated corpus of over USD 5 billion (approximately ₹40,000 crore) for scientific mine closure and progressive mine closure, demonstrating its long-term commitment to environmentally responsible mining, ecological restoration and the socio-economic development of mining-affected regions.

Highlighting the transformative potential of scientific mine closure, the Minister said reclaimed mine lands across the country are being repurposed for renewable energy, eco-tourism, water conservation, agriculture, community infrastructure and sustainable livelihood generation, citing successful examples from West Bokaro, Bishrampur and the Viveknagar Solar Project. Referring to global best practices, particularly Germany's experience in mine closure and Just Transition, he said the implementation agreement between the Ministry of Coal and GIZ would combine Germany's expertise with India's scale, policy leadership and implementation capacity to create an Indian model of mine closure that integrates environmental protection, technological innovation, community participation and economic opportunity. Emphasising that successful mine closure requires the collective efforts of governments, industry, academia, civil society, local communities and international partners, he expressed confidence that together they would transform every closed mine into a source of hope, opportunity and prosperity for future generations.

Addressing the gathering, Dr. Jitendra Singh, Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Science & Technology and Earth Sciences, and Minister of State in the Prime Minister's Office, Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions, Department of Atomic Energy and Department of Space, remarked that "the time has come to change the phrase from mine closure to mine of wealth." He lauded the Ministry of Coal for its progressive policies on scientific mine closure, stating that the successful closure of 42 coal mines demonstrates how degraded mine lands can be transformed into productive assets through ecological restoration, afforestation, water conservation, renewable energy and other sustainable community-centric initiatives. He noted that these efforts embody the principles of the circular economy by turning waste into wealth. Appreciating the India–Germany partnership and increasing private sector participation in scientific mine closure, he reaffirmed India's commitment to achieving its Net Zero target by 2070.

In his address, Shri Satish Chandra Dubey, Minister of State for Coal and Mines, emphasised that the Ministry of Coal is committed to ensuring that the benefits of mining extend far beyond coal production and translate into meaningful improvements in the lives of people living in coal-bearing regions. He said the Ministry's approach to scientific mine closure is guided by the principle that environmental restoration and socio-economic development must progress together. Referring to the Indo-German technical cooperation initiative, he said the partnership will strengthen institutional capacities, facilitate the exchange of global expertise and support the adoption of best practices in scientific mine closure and sustainable post-mining development, enabling closed mines to be transformed into assets that promote ecological restoration, sustainable livelihoods and long-term regional development.

Highlighting the significance of Coal NEER and the Tripartite MoUs under the Jharia Master Plan, Shri Dubey said these initiatives reflect the Ministry's commitment to translating sustainable mining practices into tangible community benefits. While Coal NEER will improve access to safe drinking water, promote efficient water resource management and generate livelihood opportunities through the active participation of local Self-Help Groups, particularly women, the Jharia initiative will complement rehabilitation with industry-linked skill development and employment opportunities, ensuring long-term socio-economic empowerment for rehabilitated families.

Addressing the gathering, Shri Vikram Dev Dutt, Secretary, Ministry of Coal, emphasised that mining must be guided by conscience, with people and their lives at the centre of development in mining regions. He described mine closure as a critical stage in the life cycle of mining, where responsible resource extraction must transition into long-term environmental restoration, land repurposing and sustainable community development. He noted that the Ministry of Coal has transformed mine closure from a regulatory requirement into a planned, people-centric process through the Mine Closure Guidelines, 2025, comprehensive frameworks and digital tools developed by the Coal Controller Organisation, ensuring that environmental rehabilitation, community participation and future land use are integrated into every stage of mine closure planning. Referring to the signing of the Implementation Agreement for the Indo-German Technical Cooperation Project on "To-be-Closed Coal Mines" under the Indo-German Green and Sustainable Development Partnership, he said the EUR 10 million grant from the Government of Germany and the European Union would enable India to adopt global best practices in scientific mine closure while reinforcing the shared vision that mine closure is not merely the end of mining, but the beginning of new opportunities for people, communities and the environment.

Highlighting the Ministry's commitment to inclusive post-mining development, the Secretary said that the Revised Jharia Master Plan goes beyond rehabilitation by creating sustainable livelihood opportunities through skill development and the establishment of a Plug-and-Play Textile Manufacturing Facility for rehabilitated families. He also highlighted the significant contribution of coal PSUs in supplying nearly 300 lakh kiloliters of treated mine water annually, benefiting around three lakh people, including the supply of drinking water to Chennai. Referring to the launch of Coal NEER, he said the initiative demonstrates how treated mine discharge water can be transformed into safe drinking water for nearby communities, exemplifying the Ministry's commitment to scientific innovation, sustainable resource management and community welfare.

Addressing the gathering, H.E. Dr. Philipp Ackermann, Ambassador of Germany to India and Bhutan, highlighted the continuing India–Germany partnership in advancing sustainable development. Referring to the Implementation Agreement between the Ministry of Coal and GIZ, he said the collaboration would facilitate the exchange of technical expertise and global best practices for scientific mine closure and sustainable mine repurposing, while further strengthening Indo-German cooperation for a just and sustainable transition in mining regions.

In her address, Dr. Ewa Suwara, Deputy Head of the EU Delegation to India said that the partnership between India and EU is founded on shared democratic values, mutual respect and a common commitment sustainable development. She said the Government of India’s commitment to sustainable closure of abandoned mines reflects a forward looking and responsible approach. She added that successful closure of mines requires long term planning, active engagement with stakeholders, investment in skill and reskill, land restoration and a firm commitment to ensure that the transition is fair and inclusive. She said that in this context, today’s implementation agreement represents a significant and practical step forward. She also said that the agreement gives concrete expression to the shared ambition and lays the foundation for tangible cooperation through technical assistance and capacity building.

The event reaffirmed the Ministry of Coal’s commitment to advancing scientific mine closure through progressive policies, technological innovation, institutional partnerships, and community-focused initiatives. Through initiatives such as AAROH, the Ministry continues to strengthen India’s framework for responsible mining, ecological restoration, and sustainable post-mining development, contributing towards the vision of Viksit Bharat.

(Release ID: 2288053)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
The Ministry of Coal released 'AAROH: Annual Report on Mine Closure,' India’s first comprehensive document on scientific mine closure and repurposing, alongside initiatives like Coalition NEER Plants for water safety and agreements with Germany's GIZ and industry partners for rehabilitation and skill development in coal districts.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **AAROH Report**: Documents scientific closure practices, detailing 42 closed coal mines with before-and-after visual documentation, QR code-linked videos, and structured reclamation outcomes.  
- **Mine Closure Targets**: India has scientifically closed 42 coal mines (33 in the last year), aiming to close 147 more within the next two to three years.  
- **International Collaboration**: Signed an Implementation Agreement with Germany’s GIZ (de-funded by BMZ and EU) for technical cooperation on mine closure, allocating EUR 10 million until 2030 over four years.  
- **Coal NEER Plants**: Virtually inaugurated by the Coal Minister, this initiative uses RO, UV, and CDI technologies to convert mine discharge into safe drinking water under the 'Coal Neer' brand.  
- **Industry Partnerships**: Tripartite MoUs signed between BCCL/JRDA and Hindalco/Rajdhani Universal Fabrics to establish skill centers and textile manufacturing hubs in Jharia, focusing on youth employment and industrial growth.  
- **Framework Development**: Highlights Ministry-led frameworks like RECLAIM, L.I.V.E.S., and SUVIKALP tools for stakeholder engagement and sustainable post-mining land use.

headline: India, Panama Chart New Maritime Partnership Focused on Logistics, Digital Innovation and Global Connectivity
fullText:
Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways

India, Panama Chart New Maritime Partnership Focused on Logistics, Digital Innovation and Global Connectivity

22 JUL 2026 8:38PM by PIB Delhi

India & Panama identify Maritime Security, Logistics, Skilling and Digital Transformation as key pillars of bilateral cooperation

Panama as a strategic gateway to Latin America, India seeks deeper collaboration in shipping, ports and maritime infrastructure

Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways (MoPSW), Sarbananda Sonowal held bilateral talks with Panama's Minister of Foreign Affairs, Javier Martínez-Acha Vásquez, to deepen maritime cooperation between the two countries, with both sides identifying logistics, shipping, maritime security, skill development and digital transformation as the key pillars of an expanded strategic partnership.

"India and Panama are natural maritime partners. By combining Panama's strategic logistics strengths with India's growing maritime capabilities, we can build resilient supply chains, strengthen global connectivity and create new opportunities for trade, investment, innovation and sustainable growth. Our shared vision is to transform this partnership into one of the key pillars of India's engagement with Latin America and the Caribbean," said Sarbananda Sonowal, Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways.

The discussions reaffirmed the long-standing maritime relationship between India and Panama, dating back to the 19th century, and recognised the significant contribution of the Indian diaspora in strengthening bilateral ties. With India emerging as a global ports leader and Panama serving as one of the world's foremost maritime logistics hubs, both countries agreed that closer collaboration and the exchange of best practices would unlock new opportunities for trade, connectivity and investment across the Americas.

During the meeting, Minister Javier Martínez-Acha Vásquez outlined four priority areas for expanding maritime cooperation between the two countries, maritime security, technical collaboration between India's Directorate General of Shipping and the Panama Maritime Authority, maritime education and skilling, and future-oriented cooperation in digital maritime transformation, including artificial intelligence, smart port logistics and maritime single-window systems.

Minister Sonowal highlighted India's rapid transformation under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi through sustained investments in ports, shipping, inland waterways, logistics, shipbuilding, cruise tourism and green maritime initiatives. He reiterated that Panama is India's natural gateway to Central America, Latin America and the Caribbean, and stressed the need to improve shipping connectivity, deepen logistics partnerships and accelerate institutional cooperation to realise the untapped potential of bilateral trade.

"Under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, India's maritime sector is undergoing a transformational journey driven by world-class infrastructure, modern logistics, green shipping and digital innovation. Our vision is not merely to build ports, but to build partnerships that connect economies and create shared prosperity. Panama, with its strategic maritime strengths, is a natural partner in this journey, and together we can shape a more resilient, efficient and future-ready global maritime ecosystem," said Sarbananda Sonowal.

Sonowal also proposed expediting discussions on a bilateral agreement on maritime transportation and port development, expanding cooperation in green ship recycling, strengthening seafarer welfare and skill development, and promoting collaboration in port modernisation, warehousing, digital innovation and maritime infrastructure. Minister Sonowal invited Panama's Minister of Maritime Affairs to participate in India Maritime Week 2026 in New Delhi as both sides agreed to carry forward discussions through stronger institutional partnerships.

(Release ID: 2288042)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
India and Panama have entered into a maritime partnership to enhance cooperation in logistics, digital innovation, and global connectivity, leveraging Panama’s strategic position as a gateway to Latin America and India’s expanding maritime infrastructure. The bilateral agreement emphasizes collaboration in maritime security, skill development, and technology-driven solutions.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Date**: 22 July 2026  
- **Key Officials**: Union Minister Sarbananda Sonowal (India) and Minister Javier Martínez-Acha Vásquez (Panama)  
- **Pillars of Cooperation**: Maritime security, logistics, skill development, and digital transformation  
- **Panama’s Role**: Recognized as a strategic logistics hub for India’s engagement with Latin America and the Caribbean  
- **India’s Initiatives Highlighted**: Investments in ports, shipping, inland waterways, logistics, shipbuilding, cruise tourism, and green maritime projects under PM Modi’s leadership  
- **Four Priority Areas Identified by Panama**:  
  - Maritime security  
  - Technical collaboration between India’s Directorate General of Shipping and Panama Maritime Authority  
  - Maritime education and skill development  
  - Digital maritime transformation (AI, smart ports, single-window systems)  
- **Proposals by India**:  
  - Expedite bilateral maritime transportation and port development agreement  
  - Expand green ship recycling cooperation  
  - Strengthen seafarer welfare and skill development  
  - Collaborate on port modernization, warehousing, and digital infrastructure  
- **Event Mentioned**: Panama invited to India Maritime Week 2026 in New Delhi  
- **Historical Context**: Maritime ties between India and Panama date back to the 19th century; Indian diaspora cited as contributing to bilateral relations
category: Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways

headline: DRDO inks two major contracts with Industry partners as part of AEW&C MK-II programme
fullText:
Ministry of Defence

DRDO inks two major contracts with Industry partners as part of AEW&C MK-II programme

22 JUL 2026 8:19PM by PIB Delhi

In a significant boost to Aatmanirbhar Bharat, Centre for Air Borne Systems (CABS), DRDO has signed a contract with AI Engineering Services Limited (AIESL), New Delhi for the conversion of six A321 Aircraft from commercial to green configuration, further leading to the development and production of Airborne Early Warning & Control (AEW&C) MKII Programme. A separate contract was inked with Adani Defence Systems and Technologies (ADSTL), Ahmedabad to engage with the company as Development Cum Production Partner (DcPP) for Mission Systems designed for the AEW&C MKII Programme.

The contracts were signed by Director CABS with CEO, AIESL and Head-Airborne Platform, ADSTL in the presence of Defence Secretary and Secretary, Department of Defence R&D and Chairman, DRDO Shri Rajesh Kumar Singh in New Delhi on July 22, 2026.

CABS, DRDO with support from ADSTL as DcPP is responsible for integration of mission systems on modified AEW&C MK II Platform, delivered by Airbus Defence & Space (Original Equipment Manufacturer). CABS and ADSTL will also carry out flight testing of the AEW&C MK II aircraft along with the Indian Air Force to obtain certification from airworthiness agencies - Centre for Military Airworthiness and Certification and Directorate General of Aeronautical Quality Assurance.

AEW&C Mk II System from DRDO with CABS as the nodal laboratory will provide enhanced endurance, advanced airborne surveillance, secure communication, improved situational awareness and enhanced command and control capabilities. This, in turn, will give a significant boost to the air defence capabilities and aid in neutralising threats across extended ranges.

The engagement of industries reaffirms the commitment of the Government to achieve self-reliance for fostering a robust ecosystem towards innovation, research and manufacturing within the country.

(Release ID: 2288009)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
The Centre for Air Borne Systems (CABS), DRDO, has signed two major contracts with industry partners to support the development and production of the Airborne Early Warning & Control (AEW&C) MK-II Programme. These agreements focus on aircraft conversion and the development of mission systems to enhance India's air defence capabilities.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
* **AIESL Contract:** AI Engineering Services Limited (AIESL) has been contracted to convert six A321 Aircraft from commercial to green configuration for the AEW&C MK-II Programme.
* **ADSTL Contract:** Adani Defence Systems and Technologies (ADSTL) has been engaged as a Development Cum Production Partner (DcPP) for mission systems designed for the programme.
* **Signing Details:** The contracts were signed in New Delhi on July 22, 2026, by the Director of CABS in the presence of Defence Secretary, Secretary of Department of Defence R&D, and Chairman of DRDO, Shri Rajesh Kumar Singh.
* **System Integration & Testing:** CABS and ADSTL will integrate mission systems on the Airbus Defence & Space platform and conduct flight testing alongside the Indian Air Force to obtain certification from the Centre for Military Airworthiness and Certification and the Directorate General of Aeronautical Quality Assurance.
* **System Capabilities:** The AEW&C MK-II system is designed to provide enhanced endurance, advanced airborne surveillance, secure communication, improved situational awareness, and enhanced command and control capabilities.

headline: NBA releases Rs 6.67 crore under Access and Benefit Sharing Mechanism for Biodiversity Conservation and Livelihood Support in 11 cotton-growing States
fullText:
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

NBA releases Rs 6.67 crore under Access and Benefit Sharing Mechanism for Biodiversity Conservation and Livelihood Support in 11 cotton-growing States

22 JUL 2026 8:03PM by PIB Delhi

The National Biodiversity Authority (NBA) has released Rs 6.67 crore under the Access and Benefit Sharing (ABS) mechanism to support biodiversity conservation, sustainable utilisation of biological resources and livelihood enhancement in 11 cotton-growing States. The disbursement demonstrates how benefits derived from the commercial utilisation of biological resources are being reinvested in biodiversity conservation and community welfare under the Biological Diversity Act.

The benefit-sharing amount of Rs 7.62 crore has been realised from M/s Bayer Science & Innovation Pvt. Ltd. (formerly Monsanto Holdings Pvt. Ltd.) for research involving 25,431 cotton (Gossypium hirsutum) varieties. Cotton occupies a unique place in India's agricultural and economic landscape, with the country serving as a global reservoir of cotton genetic diversity that supports nearly 4-5 crore people across the value chain. Since the biological resources were accessed through traders and intermediaries, identifying individual conservers or benefit claimers was not feasible.

To address such situations, the NBA, with the support of an Expert Committee, has developed transparent modalities for the utilisation of ABS amounts. Where the geographical occurrence of a biological resource can be reliably established using published records, the corresponding ABS amount is allocated to the concerned State Biodiversity Boards (SBBs) for biodiversity conservation, sustainable utilisation and socio-economic development of the concerned areas. Where the source cannot be established, the funds are retained in the National Biodiversity Fund and utilised in accordance with Section 27 of the Biological Diversity Act for conservation and sustainable use of biological resources across the country.

Applying these modalities, the NBA has allocated Rs 6.67 among eleven cotton-growing States: Maharashtra (Rs 2.41 crore), Gujarat (Rs 1.38 crore), Telangana (Rs 1.06 crore), Karnataka (Rs 0.40 crore), Rajasthan (Rs 0.36 crore), Madhya Pradesh (Rs 0.31 crore), Andhra Pradesh (Rs 0.23 crore), Haryana (Rs 0.23 crore), Odisha (Rs 0.14 crore), Punjab (Rs 0.09 crore) and Tamil Nadu (Rs 0.06 crore). The remaining amount (Rs 0.95 crore) has been earmarked for the NBA as per the Biological Diversity Rules.

The allocation is based on the geographical distribution of cotton cultivation using data from the All India Coordinated Research Project on Cotton (AICRP), ICAR, and is proportional to each State's area under cotton cultivation, ensuring a transparent, scientific and equitable distribution of ABS benefits.

The allocation will support on-farm and ex-situ conservation of biological resources, documentation and updation of People's Biodiversity Registers, ecosystem restoration, strengthening of Biodiversity Heritage Sites, documentation of traditional knowledge, biodiversity research, development of digital biodiversity databases, capacity building of Biodiversity Management Committees, awareness programmes and livelihood enhancement. These investments will help conserve valuable genetic traits for future crop improvement while empowering local communities as custodians of India's agrobiodiversity.

The initiative also establishes a transparent and science-based model for distributing ABS benefits where biological resources are sourced through complex supply chains. By linking benefit-sharing to the verified geographical occurrence of biological resources, it ensures that ABS reaches biodiversity-rich regions even when individual beneficiaries cannot be identified.

With this disbursement, the total ABS funds released by the NBA have reached Rs 152.24 crore. The initiative demonstrates how the ABS mechanism transforms the commercial utilisation of biological resources to combine economy, ecology and equity. It also contributes to the implementation of the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework, particularly Target 13 on the fair and equitable sharing of benefits arising from the utilisation of genetic resources, while supporting Target 4 on conserving genetic diversity.

The National Biodiversity Authority is a statutory body established under the Biological Diversity Act, 2002, functioning under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change. It regulates access to India's biological resources and associated knowledge and implements the Nagoya Protocol's principle of fair and equitable sharing of benefits.

(Release ID: 2287985)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW

The National Biodiversity Authority (NBA) has released Rs 6.67 crore under the Access and Benefit Sharing (ABS) mechanism for biodiversity conservation, sustainable utilisation of biological resources, and livelihood enhancement in 11 cotton-growing States.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS

* The NBA has released Rs 6.67 crore under the ABS mechanism for biodiversity conservation, sustainable utilisation of biological resources, and livelihood enhancement.
* The amount was realised from M/s Bayer Science & Innovation Pvt. Ltd. (formerly Monsanto Holdings Pvt. Ltd.) for research involving 25,431 cotton (Gossypium hirsutum) varieties.
* The geographical distribution of the ABS amount was allocated among 11 cotton-growing States based on the geographical distribution of cotton cultivation using data from the All India Coordinated Research Project on Cotton (AICRP), ICAR.
* The allocation is as follows:
  * Maharashtra - Rs 2.41 crore
  * Gujarat - Rs 1.38 crore
  * Telangana - Rs 1.06 crore
  * Karnataka - Rs 0.40 crore
  * Rajasthan - Rs 0.36 crore
  * Madhya Pradesh - Rs 0.31 crore
  * Andhra Pradesh - Rs 0.23 crore
  * Haryana - Rs 0.23 crore
  * Odisha - Rs 0.14 crore
  * Punjab - Rs 0.09 crore
  * Tamil Nadu - Rs 0.06 crore
* The remaining amount (Rs 0.95 crore) has been earmarked for the NBA as per the Biological Diversity Rules.
* The total ABS funds released by the NBA have reached Rs 152.24 crore.
category: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

headline: Centre and States are equal partners in building a future-ready education system for Viksit Bharat 2047 – Shri Dharmendra Pradhan
fullText:
Ministry of Education

Centre and States are equal partners in building a future-ready education system for Viksit Bharat 2047 – Shri Dharmendra Pradhan

Our collective success will be measured by the learning outcomes we achieve and the opportunities we create for every child – Union Minister for Education

Shri Dharmendra Pradhan, chairs the two-day Conclave of State and UT Education Ministers to review Centrally Sponsored Schemes of the Department of School Education and Literacy

22 JUL 2026 8:02PM by PIB Delhi

Union Minister for Education, Shri Dharmendra Pradhan today said that the Centre and the States are equal partners in strengthening school education. He was chairing the two-day Conclave of State and UT Education Ministers to review Centrally Sponsored Schemes of the Department of School Education and Literacy (DoSEL), Ministry of Education, at the ICAR National Agricultural Science Complex (NASC), New Delhi.

In his address, Shri Pradhan emphasised that the Conclave provides a valuable platform for the Centre and the States and UTs to strengthen cooperation, share best practices, and accelerate the effective implementation of Centrally Sponsored Schemes in line with the vision of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020. He said the deliberations would further reinforce the collective commitment towards building a future-ready, inclusive and high-quality school education system and contribute to the upcoming PRAGATI review chaired by the Prime Minister, Shri Narendra Modi.

Shri Pradhan highlighted the progress made in school education through initiatives such as Samagra Shiksha, PM SHRI, NIPUN Bharat, PM POSHAN and ULLAS, and called upon the States and UTs to continue working together to strengthen foundational literacy and numeracy, empower teachers, leverage technology and AI, promote innovation, and ensure the holistic development of every learner towards the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047. The Minister urged the states to promote the mother tongue of the child and multilingualism to strengthen foundational learning and improve learning outcomes.

Addressing the Conclave, Minister of State for Education and Skill Development and Entrepreneurship Shri Jayant Chaudhary, emphasised on the importance of collaborative efforts between the Centre and States and UTs to strengthen school education and improve educational outcomes.

The Conclave aims to advance the shared vision of an equitable, inclusive and quality school education system, contributing to the realisation of the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047. The sessions focussed on strengthening the implementation of Centrally Sponsored Schemes and improving educational outcomes. Education Ministers of States and UTs reaffirmed the importance of Centre–State collaboration, knowledge sharing and the shared commitment to ensuring equitable, inclusive and quality education for every learner.

The first day featured presentations by Additional Secretary, DoSEL, Shri Dheeraj Sahu, on Samagra Shiksha and issues for consideration under the PRAGATI framework, and Deputy Director General, DoSEL, Dr. Pankaj K. P. Shreyaskar, on the status of educational indicators in States and UTs. Joint Secretary, DoSEL, Dr. Amarpreet Duggal, and Economic Advisor, DoSEL ,Ms. A. Srija also participated in the proceedings along with senior officials from the Union Ministry of Education and the States.

LIVE | Addressing Conclave of State/UT Education Ministers' at New Delhi https://t.co/FJpflWWYzy

— Dharmendra Pradhan (@dpradhanbjp) July 22, 2026

(Release ID: 2287997)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
Union Minister for Education Shri Dharmendra Pradhan chaired a two-day Conclave of State and UT Education Ministers to review Centrally Sponsored Schemes of the Department of School Education and Literacy (DoSEL), emphasizing Centre-State collaboration to build a future-ready education system aligned with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Event Details**:  
  - Two-day Conclave held on July 22, 2026, at ICAR National Agricultural Science Complex (NASC), New Delhi.  
  - Chaired by Union Minister Shri Dharmendra Pradhan.  
  - Participated by Education Ministers of States and UTs, along with senior officials from DoSEL and the Union Ministry of Education.  

- **Initiatives Highlighted**:  
  - Progress under Samagra Shiksha, PM SHRI, NIPUN Bharat, PM POSHAN, and ULLAS.  
  - Focus on foundational literacy and numeracy, teacher empowerment, technology/AI integration, innovation, and holistic learner development.  

- **Policy and Vision Alignment**:  
  - Conclave supports the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047 and preparation for the upcoming PRAGATI review chaired by PM Shri Narendra Modi.  
  - Emphasis on promoting mother tongue and multilingualism to enhance learning outcomes.  

- **Collaborative Commitment**:  
  - States and UTs reaffirmed commitment to equitable, inclusive, and quality education.  
  - Sessions aimed at strengthening implementation of Centrally Sponsored Schemes and improving educational indicators.  

- **Stakeholder Participation**:  
  - Presentations by DoSEL officials, including Additional Secretary Shri Dheeraj Sahu, Deputy Director General Dr. Pankaj K. P. Shreyaskar, Joint Secretary Dr. Amarpreet Duggal, and Economic Advisor Ms. A. Srija.  
  - Minister of State for Education Shri Jayant Chaudhary underscored the importance of collaborative efforts to improve educational outcomes.

headline: Government Promotes Re-Employment Opportunities For Senior Citizens Through National Career Service (NCS) Portal
fullText:
Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment

Government Promotes Re-Employment Opportunities For Senior Citizens Through National Career Service (NCS) Portal

Population Of Persons Aged 60 Years And Above Expected To Increase To 20.8 Per Cent By 2050, To Further Reach 36 Per Cent By The End Of The Century: Union Minister For Social Justice And Empowerment Dr Virendra Kumar

NCS Portal Hosts 1.68 Lakh Suitable Vacancies For Senior Citizen Candidates, 1.89 Lakh Senior Citizen Job Seekers Registered On NCS Portal

22 JUL 2026 7:46PM by PIB Delhi

The Central Government has reaffirmed its commitment to promote active ageing and enhancing employment opportunities for senior citizens through the National Career Service (NCS) Portal. The dedicated Senior Citizen Re-employment Initiative has witnessed significant growth in registrations, employer participation and job opportunities, enabling experienced professionals to continue contributing to the nation's workforce.

This information was given by Union Minister for Social Justice and Empowerment Dr. Virendra Kumar in Rajya Sabha while replying to the question by Member of the House Shri Rajinder Gupta.

The Minister informed the House that the Government has taken note of the findings of the UNFPA-India's India Ageing Report 2023, which projects a significant rise in the country's elderly population. He said the report suggests that the population of persons aged 60 years and above is expected to increase from 10.4 per cent in 2022 to 20.8 per cent by 2050, and further reach 36 per cent by the end of the century.

Dr Kumar added that the report also highlights the need for policies that ensure livelihood opportunities and structured re-employment avenues to provide income security for elderly persons, particularly those working in the unorganised sector.

The Minister further informed that under the NCS project, the Government has approved 407 Model Career Centres (MCCs) in collaboration with States and institutions. These centres connect youth and other job seekers, including senior citizens with employment opportunities through job fairs, the NCS Portal, career counselling and training.

Dr Kumar stated that the National Career Service is playing a pivotal role in supporting India's vision of a skilled, employable and future-ready workforce adding that the platform evolving through AI-driven services, greater convergence and enhanced citizen-centric offerings, is expected to strengthen the country's modern employment services framework and contribute to inclusive economic growth and employment generation.

The Minister said that since the inception of the NCS Portal in 2015, more than 6.46 crore job seekers and 58.76 lakh employers have registered on the portal, including 1.89 lakh job seekers above the age of 60 years.

Pertinently, the portal currently hosts 1.68 lakh vacancies identified as suitable for candidates above the age of 60 years. In addition, more than 2.29 crore job seekers, including senior citizens, have been shortlisted by employers through the portal across the country.

PR-026-TAR/KV/GA

(Release ID: 2287971)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
The Government is expanding employment opportunities for senior citizens through its National Career Service (NCS) portal, following projections that the elderly population will rise to 20.8 % by 2050 and Miriam 36 % by 2100.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  

- UNFPA‑India Ageing Report 2023 projects 60+ population at **20.8 % by 2050** and **36 % by 2100**.  
- **407 Model Career Centres (MCCs)** approved in collaboration with states and institutions.  
- NCS portal (established 2015) has:  
  - **>6.46 crore** job seekers registered  
  - **58.76 lakh** employers registered  
  - **1.89 lakh**-nowrap **60‑plus** job seekers registered  
- Portal currently hosts **1.68 lakh** vacancies suitable for candidates aged 60 and above.  
- Employers have shortlisted **>2.29 crore** job seekers, including seniors, through the portal.

headline: PM Chairs High-Level Meeting of Secretaries to Review Progress of Rural Development, Agriculture, Social Welfare and Allied Sectors towards Viksit Bharat Goals
fullText:
Prime Minister's Office

PM Chairs High-Level Meeting of Secretaries to Review Progress of Rural Development, Agriculture, Social Welfare and Allied Sectors towards Viksit Bharat Goals

PM emphasises that the welfare of citizens must remain at the heart of all reforms

PM Stresses the Need to Evolve Governance Processes in Line with Changing Times

PM Calls for a Whole-of-Government Approach and breaking silos to achieve the nation's development goals

PM says that dream to build a Viksit Bharat should become a Jan Andolan and Youth should be it’s foundation

PM underscores the Need for Coordinated Action to Combat Drug Abuse and Empower Youth

PM Stresses Integrated Approach to Position India as a Global Sports Hub

PM Highlights Transformative Potential of PM-KUSUM and Need for Global Skill Mapping

Secretaries share their experiences and perspectives on various initiatives and challenges in their respective domains

22 JUL 2026 7:37PM by PIB Delhi

Prime Minister today chaired a high-level meeting with Secretaries to review progress of rural development, agriculture, social welfare and allied sectors towards Viksit Bharat Goals, at his residence at 7, Lok Kalyan Marg earlier today.

Prime Minister emphasised that the welfare of citizens must remain at the heart of all reforms. He said that reforms designed around people's needs should gather real momentum and deliver meaningful transformation. He emphasised that the objective of reforms is not merely to improve the functioning of the Government, but to accelerate India's journey towards becoming a developed nation.

Prime Minister highlighted that governance systems are witnessing rapid changes and stressed the importance of adapting and strengthening governance processes to effectively meet the needs of a dynamic and evolving environment. He suggested undertaking a comprehensive mapping of emerging requirements across various Ministries and Departments.

Prime Minister stressed the need for a whole-of-government approach to achieve the nation's development goals. He further emphasised that the dream to build a Viksit Bharat should become a Jan Andolan and that India's youth should form the foundation of this vision.

Prime Minister underscored the importance of tackling the serious issue of drug abuse among youth. He suggested that efforts should be made to develop an institutional mechanism to break departmental silos and foster greater inter-departmental coordination and convergence in youth-centric initiatives.

Prime Minister further emphasized the need to adopt an integrated approach for the development of sports. He noted that, with sports tourism emerging as a key focus area, India should aspire to host major international sporting events to establish itself as a global sports hub. He stressed the importance of promoting technology and fostering innovation through startups in the sports sector.

Prime Minister highlighted that the widespread adoption of initiatives such as the PM KUSUM Scheme by farmers can bring transformative changes to the agriculture sector. He further stressed the need for a global mapping of skill requirements to ensure better alignment with international opportunities and future needs.

The Secretaries shared their experiences and perspectives on various initiatives and challenges in their respective domains. The Prime Minister shared his suggestions and his vision for the future course of action. The meeting was marked by comprehensive and productive discussions across the sectors, focusing on effective implementation, greater convergence and accelerated progress towards the goals of Viksit Bharat.

The meeting was attended by the Cabinet Secretary, Secretaries of key Ministries as well as senior officials from the Prime Minister's Office.

(Release ID: 2287957)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a high-level meeting with Secretaries to review progress in rural development, agriculture, social welfare, and allied sectors toward Viksit Bharat goals, emphasizing citizen-centric reforms, governance adaptation, and inter-departmental coordination. The meeting focused on accelerating development initiatives, addressing drug abuse, promoting sports, and leveraging technology and global skill mapping.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Date/Location**: The meeting was held on 22 July 2026 at 7, Lok Kalyan Marg, New Delhi.  
- **Core Focus Areas**: Rural development, agriculture, social welfare, and allied sectors aligned with Viksit Bharat objectives.  
- **Reform Emphasis**: Reforms must prioritize citizen welfare and deliver meaningful transformation, not just improve government functioning.  
- **Governance Adaptations**: Need for comprehensive mapping of emerging requirements across ministries to strengthen dynamic governance systems.  
- **Jan Andolan Vision**: Viksit Bharat should evolve into a people-driven movement with youth as its foundation.  
- **Drug Abuse Tackling**: Institutional mechanisms to enhance inter-departmental coordination in youth-centric initiatives.  
- **Sports Development**: Integrated approach to promote sports tourism, host international events, and foster innovation through startups.  
- **Agriculture Initiatives**: PM-KUSUM Scheme highlighted for transformative potential in the agriculture sector.  
- **Skill Alignment**: Global mapping of skill requirements to align with international opportunities and future needs.  
- **Attendance**: Included Cabinet Secretary, Secretaries of key Ministries, and senior PMO officials. Secretaries shared domain-specific experiences and challenges.  
- **Outcome**: Discussions focused on implementation, convergence, and accelerating progress toward Viksit Bharat goals.

headline: Prime Minister pays homage to Chandra Shekhar Azad on his birth anniversary
fullText:
Prime Minister's Office

Prime Minister pays homage to Chandra Shekhar Azad on his birth anniversary

23 JUL 2026 8:22AM by PIB Delhi

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi today paid homage to Chandra Shekhar Azad on his birth anniversary. He stated that the fearless courage and unwavering patriotism of Chandra Shekhar Azad occupy a proud place in the history of India’s freedom struggle.

Shri Modi noted that he motivated countless young Indians to devote themselves to the nation, adding that his resolve continues to inspire generations.

In a post on X, the Prime Minister shared:

"Paying homage to the brave Chandra Shekhar Azad on his birth anniversary. His fearless courage and unwavering patriotism occupy a proud place in the history of India’s freedom struggle. He motivated countless young Indians to devote themselves to the nation. His resolve continues to inspire generations."

Paying homage to the brave Chandra Shekhar Azad on his birth anniversary. His fearless courage and unwavering patriotism occupy a proud place in the history of India’s freedom struggle. He motivated countless young Indians to devote themselves to the nation. His resolve continues…

— Narendra Modi (@narendramodi) July 23, 2026

(Release ID: 2288115)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
Prime Minister Narendra Modi paid homage to Chandra Shekhar Azad on his birth anniversary, emphasizing his courage and patriotism as sources of inspiration for youth and generations in India's freedom struggle.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi honored Chandra Shekhar Azad on July 23, 2026, his birth anniversary.  
- Modi highlighted Azad’s "fearless courage" and "unwavering patriotism" in India’s freedom struggle.  
- The Prime Minister shared a message on X (formerly Twitter) stating Azad’s legacy continues to motivate young Indians to serve the nation.  
- The post included the quote: "His resolve continues to inspire generations."  
- The announcement was released via PIB Delhi with Release ID: 2288115.

Equally inspiring were his efforts to make our culture popular and further the spirit of brotherhood. His writings gave people renewed confidence and furthered a spirit of pride."

headline: Prime Minister shares a Sanskrit Subhashitam, highlights the profound cosmic connection between the individual soul and the universal consciousness
fullText:
Prime Minister's Office

Prime Minister shares a Sanskrit Subhashitam, highlights the profound cosmic connection between the individual soul and the universal consciousness

23 JUL 2026 7:50AM by PIB Delhi

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi shared a Sanskrit Subhashitam today, highlighting the profound cosmic connection between the individual soul and the universal consciousness.

The Prime Minister wrote on X;

"यश्चायमस्मिन्नाकाशे तेजोमयोऽमृतमयः पुरुषः सर्वानुभूः।

यश्चायमस्मिन्नात्मनि तेजोमयोऽमृतमयः पुरुषः सर्वानुभूः॥"

The powerful, eternal reality that exists in the vast sky or space is also present within our own soul. Therefore, it is said: whatever exists within the individual being, in every particle, also exists in the universe.

यश्चायमस्मिन्नाकाशे तेजोमयोऽमृतमयः पुरुषः सर्वानुभूः।

यश्चायमस्मिन्नात्मनि तेजोमयोऽमृतमयः पुरुषः सर्वानुभूः॥ pic.twitter.com/dn8bAG2qFZ

— Narendra Modi (@narendramodi) July 23, 2026

(Release ID: 2288112)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
Prime Minister Narendra Modi shared a Sanskrit Subhashitam to illustrate the cosmic connection between the individual soul and the universal consciousness.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
* **Message Content:** The post emphasizes that the eternal reality present in space also exists within the individual soul, suggesting that what exists in every particle of an individual also exists in the universe.
* **Medium:** The message was shared via the Prime Minister's X (formerly Twitter) account.
* **Date of Release:** July 23, 2026.
* **Sanskrit Verse:** "यश्चायमस्मिन्नाकाशे तेजोमयोऽमृतमयः पुरुषः सर्वानुभूः।यश्चायमस्मिन्नात्मनि तेजोमयोऽमृतमयः पुरुषः सर्वानुभूः॥"
